1.12 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the PPE including
the identification of ongoing projects recorded under the CIP accounts
and determination of non-existing PPE or Infrastructure Projects;
b. City Engineering Office (CEO) to coordinate with the City Accounting
Office regarding the status of the CIP projects and determine whether
there are completed projects from prior years that were not transferred
to the appropriate PPE accounts;
c. City Accountant to:
c.1 Take up the necessary entries to adjust the PPE and CIP accounts
based on the Certificates of Project Completion and Acceptance
submitted by the CEO; and
c.2 Drop the unreconciled lumped amounts shown as beginning balances
without available details from the books of accounts, if warranted.

2.7 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to secure the pertinent
supporting documents on the liquidation of the various fund transfers
totaling ₱14.630 million and prepare the necessary journal entries to close
the Due to NGAs account for those fund transfers verified as fully
liquidated to source NGAs based on the supporting documents.

4.11 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:
a. Require all requisitioning offices to timely prepare and submit the SSMI to the
CAO for the preparation of JEVs in recognizing expenses for the supplies
already consumed or distributed;
b. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory count of
supplies and prepare and submit the RPCI not later than July 31 and January
31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Direct the City Accountant to reconcile the accounting records of Inventories
with the RPCI.

5.10 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary
adjusting entries for the outstanding reconciling items to ensure that the
Cash in Bank – LCCA and other affected accounts reflect accurate and
reliable balances.

Submit to the Office of the Auditor duly approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the specific assignments/ duties of the Committee Members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory-taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory. Section 5.11 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 required that a copy of the duly approved PIP shall be submitted to COA at least ten (10) days before the scheduled date of inventory;

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the outstanding reconciling items in the books of accounts after their details have been identified and the required supporting documents have been located, to reflect the correct and reliable balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts.

c. Stop granting additional cash advance for officers and employees with unsettled cash advances and formulate Cash Advances Agreement Form duly notarized and require any accountable officers and employees who are granted of cash advance to fill it up before releasing the check.

Gather all relevant documents related to the acquisition of these lands (e.g., deeds of donation, titles under purchase agreements, expropriation documents). For each prioritized parcel, initiate the titling process which may include preparing and filing the necessary applications with the Land Registration Authority (LRA), securing certifications and clearances from relevant agencies (e.g., DENR, Bureau of Lands), addressing any legal issues or disputes related to the ownership of the land, engaging surveyors, appraisers, or other professionals as needed, coordinating with the City Assessor's Office to ensure proper recording of the titled lands. The City exert effort to collect long outstanding cash advances from retired or separated City employees. If this is not possible, the City Accountant may request for the write off of dormant accounts following the procedures under COA Circular No. 92-391 dated November 25, 1992; and

Henceforth, the City adhere to the provisions of Memorandum Circular No. 155 of the Office of the President as embodied under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 by requiring City employees with outstanding cash advances to settle their accountabilities before clearance for transfer or resignation is granted.

We recommend that CENRO resolve the garbage/trash accumulation problem by hiring and training additional personnel to operate the site and re-orient existing regular personnel. Strict compliance of Section 42 (m and n) of RA No. 9003 and Solid Waste Management Ordinance No. 2013-007 is enjoined.

We recommended that the City Mayor assign personnel to take charge in the monitoring and collection of loan receivables. Moreover, the Accounting Office prepare and furnish a monthly aging schedule to the assigned personnel to facilitate monitoring and collection of the loan receivables from cooperatives and other beneficiaries.

It is recommended that EEO issue demand letters to all market stall-owners with long overdue accounts.  Also, execute contract of lease for market stalls and properly monitor daily/weekly/monthly collections of rentals payments.
We   recommend   that    the cash reward of                   P50,000.00 each or a total of P200,000.00 be refunded by the four former councilors.

We   recommend that the   City   Mayor   should instruct the City Accountant to determine the collectibility of    the receivable. Further, management must institute specific courses of action to collect the   same     if collection of the amount is still probable.   Otherwise, provide allowance for the said receivables based on existing regulations.

We recommended that:

The City Treasurer direct the personnel in charge in the preparation of Report of Check Issued (RCI) to completely record and book all checks issued during the month including cancelled checks and consequently submit the same to the Office of the City Accountant;

The City Accountant shall draw journal voucher to adjust stale checks totaling ₱766,081.96 to the surplus account of the City pursuant to Section 97 of PD No. 1445; and

The City Accountant direct the staff in charge in the preparation of BRS to immediately prepare journal voucher for all reconciling items to adjust and correct the GL balance of Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to:

Direct the CPDO and City Engineer and other implementing officers to fast track LDRRMF projects being implemented by their office; and 

Require the Head of LDDRMO to maintain complete records/file on the receipt and utilization of the LDRRMF and to submit quarterly report to the Audit Team.

We recommended that:
The CPDC and other officials involved in the planning ensure that programs, projects and activities to be included in the 20% DF are within the scope of JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 or directly aligned  to the development projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and

The City Budget Officer and the City Accountant must ascertain proper charging of expenses to the 20% DF and must not allow expenditure items not in conformity with the guidelines as provided under JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that:
The City Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team a copy of the perfected contract within five working days upon approval together with the supporting documents for review pursuant to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and

The City Mayor and the City Accountant ensure that payments to contractors of infrastructure projects are supported with all the required documents before payments are certified/approved.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the City Mayor create a special task force, to closely coordinate with the City Legal Office that will work on the titling of all             real properties donated or acquired by the City Government which has remained untitled to support the balance of Land account in the financial statements.
It is further recommended that the GSO and the City Legal Officer exert effort to locate all titles of land owned by the City and process the                titling of land acquired through donation or other modes of acquisition with the Land Registration Authority, where necessary.

It is recommended that the City Accountant ensure that AP recorded in the books are valid claims of city officials, employees, suppliers or contractors.   Those not supported with valid claims which has been outstanding for two years or more must be reverted to unappropriated surplus pursuant to          Section 159 of GAAM, Volume I.
Further, the Accounting Office conduct regular monitoring and reconciliation of the payable account and ensure that all payables are backed up by cash in order to present a reliable and more accurate balance of said account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Accountant and/or the City Treasurer:
In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks especially those checks which have been outstanding for over two years and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
Hereafter, the two offices coordinate, monitor checks already issued but remain outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month before the check becomes stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Evaluate payables included in the schedule of Accounts Payable to determine if they are valid obligations of the City to suppliers/contractors, City officials/employees and other payees;
Prepare adjusting entry for payables which did not pass the recognition criteria of a liability as defined under MNGAS, Volume I and the related regulation provided under Section 157 of GAAM, Volume I by:
Debit 		Accounts Payable
Credit			Due to Officers and Employees
Due to other LGUs
Prior Period Adjustments
Recognize liability and record under accounts payable only when; goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Record transactions to accounts payable only when supported by sufficient evidence and when there is proper authority for its incurrence in conformity with Section 157 of GAAM, Volume 1; and
Propose to the Sangguniang Panlungsod upon approval of the City Mayor the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund Accounts Payable, which are outstanding in the books for more than two years and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record in consonance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Accountant to prepare adjusting entry for payables representing unpaid 20% Development Fund which did not pass the recognition criteria of a liability as defined under MNGAS, Volume I with the following entries:
Debit		Accounts Payable
Credit	Appropriate Asset accounts
(based on the original entry)
Prior period Adjustments for expense account
Hereafter, the City Accountant to classify the unexpended yearly balances of the 20% DF at the end of the year as 20% DF-Continuing Appropriations;
Instruct the City Accountant to discontinue the practice of recording the unexpended balance of the 20% DF to Accounts Payable since such action is not founded on existing regulation; and
Direct the City Development Council to decide on the use of the accumulated unexpended balance of the 20% DF totaling ₱79,029,783.50 which shall be utilized in accordance with the requirements of Sections 2.2 and 2.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Accountant to –
Immediately prepare adjusting entries for the improper treatment of the unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE by:
Debit		Accounts Payable
Credit		Appropriate Asset/Expense Accounts
(based on the original entry)
Prior Period Adjustments
Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund books every end of the year pursuant to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
Record in the AP the obligations under CDRRMF if such liabilities pass the recognition criteria of a liability as defined under MNGAS, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor –
Enforce the Punong Barangays to submit the Projects Proposals/Program of Works to support the proposed projects for inclusion in the City Development Assistance under the 20% DF to facilitate immediate implementation of their projects;
The CPDC or the City Engineer or proponents of the projects to prepare plans and specifications/programs of works for all approved projects included in the 20% DF plan; and
The CPDC conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all projects funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the CPDC, City Engineer and CRDDMO Head to implement all planned programs, activities and projects contained in the LDRRM Plan for succeeding years based on the timeframes indicated in the said Plan so that all targeted outputs are accomplished/completed; and
Direct the CDRRM Council to prioritize essential projects to be included in the LDRRM plan so that realignment of projects could be avoided.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the City Treasurer prepare and submit actual Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on an updated Assessment Roll pursuant to City Revenue Code and Section 212 of RA No. 7160; and
Require the City Accountant to set up in the books RPT/SET receivables based on updated Assessment Roll pursuant to the requirements of Section 19.b, Chapter 3 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, for fair presentation of the receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct all concerned offices to maximize local revenue options granted to LGUs under RA No. 7160, specifically the appraisal, assessment and collection of real property taxes so that it will not be dependent on its IRA share and resort to loans and borrowings to finance development projects.
Instruct the City Assessor to expedite the preparation of the schedule of fair market values of real properties within the jurisdiction of the City based on updated Assessment Roll, submit the same to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for adoption of the proposed SMV after a public hearing is conducted and to undertake the required general revision of property assessment pursuant to Section 219 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Ensure maximum utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities as planned in order to attain the GAD objectives;
Ascertain that all programs, projects and activities included in the GAD plan and budget are based on identified Gender issues and concerns and make sure that expenses incurred should be within its budget and indicate the funding sources of the excess expenditures over the budget and justification for not observing the budget; and
Direct the Chairperson, GFPS TWG to strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget/Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We    recommend that  the Accountant    set up Special    Account   in  the General    Fund   for    the Housing   Program   of the City,    establish           the receivable       from      the recipients   of  the  housing lots,    adjust   the     asset account  equivalent  to the cost  of the  paid-up   lots and  transfer     the    total amount collected from the Trust Fund to the  Special Account  in  the   General Fund.  She should likewise maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Special account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor must adhere to the regulations on the requisition of supplies as provided in Section 358 of RA 7160 by requiring the General Service Officer to fill the supplies requirement of the City. Moreover, require the GSO to accept the items delivered before inspection is conducted pursuant to to Section 118 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

    Considering the materiality of the amount involved, it is imperative that the City Mayor must ensure that issuance of supplies to end users should be properly reported following the procedures prescribed in Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and adequate control measures must be instituted to avoid wastage of government resources.
observations.managementAction: Supplies are immediately issued to end-users
observations.reason: The City has no stockroom for supplies

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Treasurer should support the Real Property Tax Receivable with a duly certified list of taxpayers and amount due and collectible Likewise, she should set a target date to complete the encoding of the taxpayers data in the computer system so she could comply with the requirement as set forth in Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I to furnish the City Accountant with said list.
observations.managementAction: The CTO will be provided with RPT data with details available upon demand
observations.reason: CTO can provide data upon demand

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant should reconcile their records (Cashbook and General/Subsidiary Ledger) at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be resolved promptly in order to present a correct balance of accountability at any given time.

 Moreover, the City Treasurer should require the Cashier to comply with the requirement that cash on hand shall be balanced with the cashbook at the end          of the day to enable the accountable officer to correct errors promptly.
observations.managementAction: Will continue of both the Cashbook and SL/GL balances
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going

observations.recommendation: We   recommend that the   City   Mayor   should instruct the City Accountant  to determine the collectibility  of    the receivable. Further, management must institute specific  courses  of action to  collect  the   same     if collection of the amount is still probable.   Otherwise, provide allowance for the said receivables based on existing regulations.
